newsSinceJun6: No MDA-specific material news since June 6. Today's -5.0% move is sector flow, not fundamentals: SpaceX priced its IPO at $135/share (~$1.75T valuation, below the ~$2T some investors expected), with the roadshow starting June 8 and listing June 12, triggering profit-taking and proxy-dumping across space names (Intuitive Machines -15%, Rocket Lab -7%, AST SpaceMobile -9%); a Blue Origin rocket explosion added sentiment pressure. MDA's latest fundamental positives (selected by BAE Systems for U.S. Space Systems Command MEO Epoch 2 constellation; Jefferies PT raise to C$51 from C$41) both landed June 2, before the window, and remain intact.

sources.label: Benzinga - Why space stocks are falling ahead of SpaceX IPO
TNW - Space stock rally cracks as SpaceX IPO nears and Blue Origin explodes
Stocktwits - Space stocks extend selloff as Jefferies courts SpaceX bears

verdicts.rankRationale: On a latent/under-owned-nodes lens, a -5% flow-driven flush from the SpaceX-IPO proxy-dumping wave with zero MDA-specific bad news improves asymmetry: the thesis (satellite backlog, robotics, geointelligence, thin U.S. coverage) is untouched and the June 2 BAE MEO Epoch 2 selection actually strengthens it. MDA is far less crowded than the U.S. space proxies (RKLB/LUNR/ASTS) bearing the brunt of the de-rating, so it is being sold on sector beta, not ownership.
verdicts.bull: BAE Systems MEO Epoch 2 selection plus a >$4B pipeline (majority satellites) validate MDA as a real production bottleneck for space-based sensing/comms; a sector flush ahead of the June 12 SpaceX listing lets buyers in below the rerating high.
verdicts.neutral: Stock is still up ~86% YTD, so part of the latent-alpha discount has already been harvested; the SpaceX IPO overhang could keep space proxies heavy through and after June 12 without changing MDA's fundamentals.
verdicts.bear: If post-IPO capital rotates durably into SpaceX itself, multiples across listed space names compress regardless of backlog; MDA also carries program risk (EchoStar loss precedent, Artemis/Canadarm3 policy sensitivity).
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of the BAE/SSC MEO Epoch 2 selection into firm contract value, plus next quarterly print confirming 40%+ revenue growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Sustained sector de-rating as SpaceX's June 12 IPO absorbs space-investor capital, dragging MDA's multiple down with crowded U.S. proxies.
